texto: Women are born to serve and breed, and when we fail at this, what else is there? What is it that we can do that men can't? We bring their screaming mouths into this world only to be told by those mouths, now grown, that we're lesser than. Only good for being on our knees, backs, or perched over stoves. We're told, You're smart for a woman, you're mouthy for a woman, you're brazen for a woman. They tell us we're dangerous and emotional, prone to hysterics like landmines, and I wonder, if this is true, why aren't they afraid?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Felicia C. Sullivan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a inteligencia. O trecho “Women are born to serve and breed, and when we fail at this, what else is there? What is it that …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que inteligencia, men, patriarchy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de patriarchy e women.
